title: Availability, Disruption Budgets, Graceful Draining, Autoscaling, and Capacity · API Object Lens: K8s Hpa Metrics Control Behavior
summary: A two-zone service scales from noisy metrics, violates its recovery target during drain, and has enough replicas but insufficient failure-domain and resource headroom. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s hpa metrics control behavior increment in availability and autoscaling operating policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Derive HPA desired replicas from requests, utilization, custom or external metrics, missing samples, tolerance, stabilization, and scale policies.
Calculate minAvailable or maxUnavailable, unhealthy eviction policy, controller scale, simultaneous drains, and voluntary versus involuntary disruption limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio

version-baseline:

  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example service has 5 replicas but all share one zone, a strict disruption budget blocks drain, and HPA oscillates because requests and metrics are wrong. Balance failure domains, PDB, rollout surge, autoscaling signals, stabilization, and capacity. Competency probe: Derive HPA desired replicas from requests, utilization, custom or external metrics, missing samples, tolerance, stabilization, and scale policies. A defensible solution applies K8s Hpa Metrics Control Behavior, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to availability and autoscaling operating policy.

content.code:
# Worked focus: k8s-hpa-metrics-control-behavior — Derive HPA desired replicas from requests, utilization, custom or external metrics, missing samples, tolerance, stabilization, and scale policies.
  # Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-availability-disruption-autoscaling-3
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-availability-disruption-autoscaling-3
    artifact: availability-policy.yaml
    decision: worked k8s availability disruption autoscaling 3
    environment: shared multi-tenant cluster under Restricted Pod Security
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      objective: 99.9-percent-availability
      replicas: 4-across-2-zones
      pdb: minAvailable-3
      hpa: autoscaling-v2-cpu-and-queue
      stabilization: bounded
      node-headroom: measured
      faults: drain-zone-loss-metrics-gap
    verify: kubectl get deployment,pdb,hpa -n intake -o json
    failure: Reject replica-count-only HA, PDB as involuntary-failure protection, limit-based HPA math, or autoscalers without capacity and feedback analysis.
    repair: Tie replicas and topology to objectives, calculate disruption and scaling, reserve headroom, inject lag and failure, and measure user-visible service.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: HPA uses CPU limits and reacts continuously without delay or stabilization. This fails the K8s Hpa Metrics Control Behavior contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
